description: Sector-specific screening controls for UK electronic communications providers, telecom networks, broadband/phone services, Ofcom General Conditions, customer protections, vulnerable users, contract information, and network/service obligations.

metadata.scope: UK sector overlay controls for Telecom Sector Compliance Overlay. Intended to be used with uk_core_control_library.
metadata.source_note: Source basis is mapped from official UK legal/regulatory sources available at rule-pack publication time. This starter pack is not legal advice and requires expert validation before production reliance.
metadata.external_knowledge_policy: No external knowledge is introduced during document evaluation. Regulatory source mappings live only in this versioned rule pack.

primary_sources.name: General Conditions that protect business customers
Rules for phone and broadband providers

limitations: Rules encode document-evidence checks, not a final legal determination.
Each rule requires validation against current UK laws, regulations, regulator notices, codes and guidance before production reliance.
UK rules may differ between England, Wales, Scotland and Northern Ireland; human reviewers must verify territorial applicability.

rules.title: Ofcom General Conditions compliance evidence is present
rules.summary: Checks for Ofcom General Conditions, communications provider, entitlement, condition compliance, or regulatory obligation evidence.

metadata.evidence_expectation: The reviewed document/document set should contain direct textual evidence for this control, or the engine should return missing/review_required according to the rule strategy.
metadata.review_guidance: Automated results are evidence-based screening signals only. A qualified UK legal/compliance reviewer must confirm applicability, legal sufficiency, and treatment of missing evidence before reliance.

rules.title: Customer contract information and summary evidence is present
rules.summary: Checks for contract information, contract summary, minimum term, termination rights, price changes, or end-of-contract notification evidence.

metadata.evidence_expectation: The reviewed document/document set should contain direct textual evidence for this control, or the engine should return missing/review_required according to the rule strategy.
metadata.review_guidance: Automated results are evidence-based screening signals only. A qualified UK legal/compliance reviewer must confirm applicability, legal sufficiency, and treatment of missing evidence before reliance.
source_basis: Ofcom guidance under General Condition C1
General Conditions that protect business customers

rules.title: Vulnerable or disabled customer protections are documented
rules.summary: Checks for vulnerable customer policy, disabled customer services, priority fault repair, text relay, or accessibility evidence.

rules.title: Network standards, interfaces, or resilience controls are documented
